programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark invites Arthur and Pauline out for an Indian meal. Sharon pays some surprise visits around the square. Ricky tries to get Bianca a job at the cafe

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Tony's family problems take their toll when he answers an urgent call from his father while he's on duty, earning a rebuke from Smithy and adding weight to Heaton's view that Sun Hill could get better value from its staff by getting rid of the expensive veterans and taking on inexperienced youngsters. Sam works with boyfriend Stuart on the case of an old lag stabbed soon after his release from prison, but she turns to old pal Phil Hunter when she discovers she's pregnant

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Michael is suspicious when Abra tries to prevent a convicted murderer receiving a liver transplant. Kyla can only stand by while other people make decisions regarding her son Max's medical treatment while in foster care. Elliot tries to avoid Lady Byrne following their kiss, but realises that it might not have been an accident after all

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Events take a tragic turn when Jeff threatens a man while on a call-out with Nikki, and the arrival of an attractive neurologist causes problems between Harry and Charlie. Colette tells Simon about the baby, but he does not want to know, and the arrival of a prostitute in A&E only complicates matters. Hospital drama, starring Kelly Harrison, Christopher Colquhoun and Elizabeth Carling

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Kaye Wragg joins the cast as PC Diane Noble, who makes a good start with the Sun Hill team when she and Smithy arrest a key figure in the crack house raid. Gina is less than impressed with Heaton's heavy-handed tactics, which leave an elderly witness fighting for her life. Sam suffers a miscarriage and realises from Stuart's reaction that he isn't ready for a long-term commitment

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: The medic resolves to track down a mysterious man who callously ignored his request to call for an ambulance - and Finlay's obsession soon leads him into a macabre world. Drama, starring David Rintoul

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Cardale is thrown into a state of shock when Alice North is brutally attacked in her own home. The shock helps push Joanna back into the arms of her ex-lover Martin Henderson. Can the pillar of the community recover from this devastating intrusion?

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James discovers the downside of life as a vet when the post-mortem results for Lord Hulton's horse arrive, while Siegfried's younger brother Tristan comes back from college and James is introduced to Mrs Pumphrey and her dog Tricki Woo

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester decides to call in the experts to deal with redecorating - but her choice is less than ideal. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom's son Geoffrey summons the courage to walk out on his wife, and moves into Bayview instead - but after a few days of his boring company Diana hatches a plan to get rid of him

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Newlyweds Fanny and Leclerc are jailed by the Germans for flying around in a bed attached to a balloon. Rene is horrified when Edith resolves to rescue them, but her plans are thwarted by a loose bedknob. Second World War comedy, starring Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: As Clegg and Truly prepare to face life without Compo, a letter arrives addressed to their late friend from his son Tom. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Tom Owen

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary investigate the mysterious death of a blind professor caught up in a bitter battle to save his beloved allotment from an unscrupulous land developer. A heady blend of weedkiller, cannabis and high finance hangs in the air as the duo search for clues - and another body is turned up before they track down the villain. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Famous thriller author Frank O'Toole is found stabbed through the heart after taking an early morning swim in the ocean. Suspicion soon falls on the victim's wife Valerie, but with eyewitnesses confirming he was swimming alone, how could anyone have got from the house to the sea without being detected? Away from the investigation, Dwayne concentrates on finding a date for a voodoo love festival. Whodunit, guest starring Simon Callow, Imogen Stubbs, and Sian Gibson of Car Shame fame

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack announces he is quitting the team, but before Sandra, Brian and Gerry can question his reasons, shadowy Whitehall figure Stephen Fisher arrives with one of his secret cases - the unsolved murder of a woman dating back more than 160 years. With just 24 hours to solve it, will the sleuths find time to get to the bottom of Jack's shock decision and persuade him to stick around?

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: An American assassin is asked to come out of retirement to kill an Englishman, and Tom is put in an intolerable position when Christine reveals sensitive information about the clandestine operation. Against her wishes, he puts the team on the case to find the would-be culprit, but his colleagues begin to doubt his judgement and motives when the trail goes cold. Crime drama, starring Matthew Macfadyen and Megan Dodds

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team flies out to Zambia to investigate the death of a female doctor who disappeared two weeks earlier. It transpires the woman was volunteering for a Mission and had believed a number of refugees died in suspicious circumstances. Leo and Nikki carry out a post-mortem, but their findings throw the case into disarray. Patrick Baladi and Robert Pugh guest star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James manages to settle into the routine of his new life - but then meets Helen, the beautiful daughter of a local farmer. Siegfried decides to bring some business-like discipline to the practice by employing the formidable Mrs Harbottle as secretary

programme.desc: William is roped in as a waiter for Hester's latest business venture, a catering company, and is distraught when one of the guests makes a play for his wife. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: A vicar suffers a nervous breakdown at a resident's funeral and Tom's son has trouble with his nymphomaniac wife. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.desc: General von Flockenstoffen decides to launch a raid to capture Churchill, leaving the Colonel, Gruber and Bertorelli to resort to desperate measures to stop him

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo's son causes chaos when he embarks on a mission to find a spare exhaust pipe for his van. Peter Sallis and Tom Owen star in the long-running comedy set in Yorkshire

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the detective drama. Agatha Christie's elderly sleuth is intrigued by the case of a businessman who died in his 'counting house' with a pocketful of rye - and as she sets out to crack the 'crime by rhyme', suspicion soon falls on the household and staff at Yew Tree Lodge. Joan Hickson, Timothy West, Peter Davison, Tom Wilkinson, Stacy Dorning, Clive Merrison and Rachel Bell star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: In their first case without trusty colleague Jack, the team members reinvestigate the disappearance of PE teacher Jason Bowe when the remains of a body are discovered near the elite public boarding school where he taught. Unfortunately, their snooping could not have come at a worse time for the staff, who are preparing to welcome a local MP to open their new computer centre - but what they do glean tells them the dead man seemed very much a loner. However, it soon turns out he got on much better with his students, forming close and inappropriate relationships with several. But could his behaviour have rattled someone enough to kill him? Susannah Harker and Nicola Walker guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Tom is accused of murdering the Chief of Defence staff, and the team have to find out what happened and whether he has really turned traitor. A confident new recruit from MI6 motivates them into action - but they are also subjected to a punitive government investigation. Starring Keeley Hawes, Peter Firth and Rupert Penry-Jones, with a guest appearance by Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The mummified remains of a woman are found during a rescue operation in a cave, and the body is discovered to be the ex-wife of a chief constable. Dalziel and Pascoe investigate, and their lives are put on the line when they brave treacherous conditions to uncover more evidence at the scene of the find. With Denis Lawson, Reece Dinsdale, Christine Tremarco and Sylivia Syms

Hospital drama, starring Simon MacCorkindale, Ashlie Walker and N'Deaye Baa-Clements

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Stalker Keith Durante is finally brought in for questioning after Kezia leans on his wife to retract the alibi she gave him. However, Jack Meadows finds the interrogation a frustrating affair and resorts to devious methods to extract a confession. A fire in a B&B alerts the Sun Hill watch to an illegal immigration operation on their patch, and Sam finally accepts she has no future with Stuart

programme.desc: Nigel becomes manager of the team. Roy leaves after Pat refuses to go on a cruise with him. Sharon gives Grant a letter from the solicitors

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: McKenna becomes obsessed with the wife of a wounded war veteran he is treating, while Finlay and Cameron are intrigued by a film star staying in a local hotel. Rural medical drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Dawn's son slips into a coma, leaving surgeons in a state of confusion as to what could have brought about his rapid deterioration. It's not long before estranged father Keith turns up on the scene, adding to the air of tension as his wife's involvement with Andrew comes to light. Medical drama, starring Gary Mavers, Haydn Gwynne and Sarah Parish

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James realises he has a rival for Helen's affections, while Siegfried reaches breaking point with Miss Harbottle. Tristan has another all-nighter with the bell ringers, leading Siegfried to believe he has too much time on his hands - so he puts him in charge of some newly acquired piglets

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Emma asks her parents to babysit while she and Peter attend a function in London. Meanwhile, Hester delivers meals on wheels to one of Sonia's old ladies but gets lost, collides with a policeman and causes a traffic jam. comedy, starring Julia MaKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom's attempts to entertain his two obnoxious grandchildren prove a washout in more ways than one - so dauntless Diana decides to teach the two underage delinquents a lesson they'll never forget. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: A medal for bravery, a suicide pill and an unlikely hiding place all add up to a confusing time for reluctant resistance fighter Rene, who determines to flee to England with Yvette instead of the two British airmen Fairfax and Carstairs

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Tom reckons he can turn Babs into a musical star so Clegg and Truly offer to collect some music for him - only to discover the 'music' is actually a large console organ. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Tom Owen

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: The beleaguered barrister puts aside his domestic problems to defend a prostitute on the charge of murdering a high-profile businessman - a tricky case made all the more difficult by the discovery that she has many enemies, all of them determined to blacken her character. Drama, starring John Thaw

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra reopens an investigation into the suspected suicide of a Foreign Office diplomat, whose body was found in a frozen London lake after she had suffered the personal tragedy of a miscarriage and the professional embarrassment of having a government laptop stolen from her home. The woman's fiance and twin sister both believe her death is connected to sensitive information held on the missing computer and blame her boss for her downfall - and when Sandra clashes with the man himself, it only makes her more determined to pursue the conspiracy theory, despite Strickland's advice to tread carefully. Simon Day and Sharon Small guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Harry activates sleeper agent Fred Roberts, who had previously been living as a renowned chemist, and uses him to infiltrate a terrorist group. However, as Fred plays his part in the elaborate sting operation, Tom begins to question the morality of MI5's actions. Meanwhile, an encounter with photographer Will North has unexpected repercussions for Zoe. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When a jockey dies at the racecourse, the detectives find themselves on the sinister side of the sport after discovering he was poisoned with a blood-thinning drug. Their investigations lead them to links with a stable hand who has been missing for over a year, a gambling lottery winner, who happens to be the racecourse's biggest client, and a mysterious vet

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Stalker Keith Durante is finally brought in for questioning after Kezia leans on his wife to retract the alibi she gave him. programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate quality produce that needs to be cooked with the respect it deserves, as they continue their culinary journey through time. The duo argue that, while convenience food serves a purpose, only the best ingredients and perfect cooking can produce something truly delicious

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple of keen horticulturalists who have £450,000 to find a home in the Yorkshire Dales, and also visits RHS Garden Harlow Carr to meet Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers have a £5,500 budget to create a dramatic Balinese garden full of greenery and tropical plants for newlyweds Peter and Samantha in Cheltenham

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades oversees proceedings as the show's experts restore an 80-year-old banjo, a pair of 1930s rollerskates and a 19th-century wooden salad bowl

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The programme hears from whistleblowers working inside the Government's new coronavirus tracking system. They are so concerned about NHS Test and Trace that they are speaking out to reveal chaos, technical problems, confusion, wasted resources and a system that does not appear to them to be working. There are also interviews with members of local public health teams, who say they have largely been ignored by the Government in favour of the private companies hired to run the new centralised tracking system

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian hosts a business meeting at home, determined to impress a council colleague with his hospitality skills - only for Max to storm in making accusations and threats. Isaac is reeling after his meeting with the school about the drugs, leading to an altercation with Phil, and Mick is disturbed to find Frankie taking photos of Ollie. Kheerat tries to explain himself to Karen, and Rainie and Stuart say their farewells on the night before their wedding

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: After a heavy night partying, Alison and Mike wake to find their house in disarray and with the clock ticking until a potentially crucial business partner comes to view the venue, the couple attempt a mass clean up. The ghosts are all acting very strangely, and Alison struggles to piece together the events of the night before to figure out what happened to them

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff: Living with Bulimia
programme.desc: The former England cricketer faces up to his eating disorder of 20 years as he challenges the stigma around the condition in men. He explores how bulimia has played a part in the course of his life, discusses his own experience in detail and meets specialists and young men with eating disorders across the UK

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Showcase for emerging talent, filmed in six different cities across the UK in outdoor locations with socially distanced audiences. The first edition is from Bristol, where local comedian Jayde Adams plays host to Mo Omar, Lauren Pattison and Tom Lucy

programme.title: Yung Filly's Celeb Lock-In
programme.desc: The host is joined by Wayne and Frankie Bridge on the game show, with the couple helping four Geordie households competing to win the grand prize

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor, writer and TV presenter Richard Ayoade takes over the reins for an extended 2019 edition of the satirical quiz, with Pointless co-host Richard Osman and comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A shocking discovery by two unarmed police officers brings a bomb disposal unit to a suburb of Merseyside. In Dorset, a patient lashes out at a paramedic on the way to hospital

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Doncaster, Kent's Herne Bay, and London's Edmonton

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: How two brothers in Wales ran their own drug organisation until getting found by police. Cameras view their two much-prized Motorcross bikes being sold at auction

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on a nurse ripping off the NHS for £1000s, and the boss of a recycling firm who helped himself to millions in government money

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Caroline Hawley help two teams look for items at Newark Antiques Fair in Nottinghamshire, before seeing if they make profits at auction. Anita Manning presents

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Colliery manager Richard Sharpe is desperate to get hold of a mysterious diary left behind by Prem's predecessor Dr Elwyn, and hires local street urchin Dan Griffiths to steal the book from the surgery. However, he underestimates Kamini's attentiveness, and the lad is caught in the act, leading to a heated confrontation. Meanwhile, the doctor has an emergency of his own to deal with when he is asked to make an unusual house call. Comedy drama, starring Mark Williams, Ayesha Dharker and Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the nation's love affair with beer and cider, revealing how at one time it was safer to drink ale than water. The duo are also given a chance to gain hands-on brewing experience at a cider festival

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple find a new home in the Norfolk countryside, and learns about the county's history with Lotus cars as their latest model is unveiled

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a practical but fun garden for a St Albans family, with play structures for the children and a place to grow fruit and vegetables

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team takes on the challenge of restoring an 18th-century French clock by a renowned maker, a 1960s radiogram, and a Victorian botanical microscope

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Part one of two. Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Drama following the interconnecting stories of the tenants of a house in Manchester divided into four flats. Gail bumps into an old friend who makes a startling observation about her marriage. Belle's ordered life is thrown into disarray when he has to look after her niece. David returns from holiday with an unexpected guest. Pregnant Hannah reconnects with the father of her child. Starring Alison Steadman, Peter Davison and Adrian Lester

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Saf and Robbie hope that dating someone new will help to put the past behind them

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Sim and Aston test whether their long distance relationship is strong enough to progress

programme.title: Michael McIntyre: Happy & Glorious
programme.desc: A chance to see a show from the popular stand-up star's 2015 tour of the UK and Ireland, recorded as-live at the O2 in London. Returning to a venue he has visited 27 times in the past, more than any other artist in the world, Michael showcases his particular brand of wit, taking aim once again at some of the absurdities of everyday life

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Staff of London Ambulance Service gear themselves up for the fall out from another day of intense politics in the city, as hundreds of thousands of protesters take to the streets. Despite all their planning in how to handle the crowds, the team in control are taken by surprise by a problem no one had foreseen - the entire computer system used to respond to 999 calls crashes. Elsewhere, staff receive a call about a patient who is not breathing, and Advanced Paramedic Kev is immediately dispatched to the cardiac arrest

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Firefighters are shocked when they are attacked by the residents of a house on fire as they try to put it out, while West Midlands Police officers patrol Birmingham on Black Friday

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters explore a bungalow near the coast in Dymchurch in Kent, a detached house in Glanamman in Carmarthenshire and a terraced house in Thornton Heath in Surrey

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in Derby hit the streets to arrest dealers making money on their patch, and money raised from selling criminals goods is used to help feed people at a pop-up restaurant

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The specialists investigate a serial insurance fraudster who tried to cash in on horrific terror attacks, and a GP surgery manager who blew NHS money at the bookmakers

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Caroline Hawley guide two teams scouring Spitalfields Market in the heart of London's East End for bargains to take to Catherine Southon's auction house. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out how the area around Spitalfields became known as Weaver's Town with TV historian Dan Cruickshank

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Kamini wants to escape from Trefelin but realises she is the only one who can help local tearaway Dan when he gets into trouble. Prem learns more about the miners' deteriorating health as Megan helps him translate the previous doctor's diary. Comedy drama set in the 1960s, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar and Ayesha Dharker

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey by exploring the history behind some of Britain's famous pies, a dish the duo have written a book about. They also find out 'who ate all the pies' on the football pitch

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a retired couple escape to the tranquillity of Norfolk after 30 years in a busy Essex town. Now that their children have flown the nest, the pair are hoping to find a country home that's full of character for their £350,000 budget. Elsewhere, Margherita discovers Norfolk's answer to the Great Barrier Reef

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden in Bournemouth for young married couple Thomas and Rhiann, who long for a family-friendly garden with a koi carp pond

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three more treasured family heirlooms, working on a Victorian railway lamp, a musical jewellery box and a Brownie toadstool

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: A party political broadcast by the Liberal Democrats

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers Dave and Harry Rich conjure up a magical fairy theme for Tom and Heather's sloping garden in Alfreton, Derbyshire, but it remains to be seen whose design will leave the couple spellbound

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Stephen Kember is given a long-mute music box that once belonged to a professional stage magician and toy experts Julia Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch get their hands on a much-loved push-along horse that has seen better days. Other items up for repair are a water-damaged penny arcade game and an old leather-backed chair

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the third episode, crew members in the capital respond to reports of an armed male and two stabbings. Elsewhere, Adam and Orani are dispatched to a long-term heroin user whose concerned mum has called for help

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: Romantic comedy anthology series. After struggling to make romantic connections in her younger years, Darby finally falls in love. Starring Anna Kendrick

programme.title: Love Life
programme.desc: A year later, Darby's life and career feel stagnant. When sparks fly with Bradley, her former boss who is recently divorced and highly successful, Darby feels pressure to revisit her half-baked dream of becoming a photographer - but soon gets swept up into girlfriend duties instead. Romantic comedy anthology series, starring Anna Kendrick

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Behind the doors of the Shankly Hotel in Liverpool, which has themed party suites so guests can celebrate important moments in their lives. But with the hotel's online rating having slipped, a new general manager has been called in to shake things up, although his changes do not go down well with the staff. Elsewhere, wedding planner Ciara helps brides-to-be Trudie and Danielle, while guest relations officer Liam looks after Laura, who has checked in to celebrate her recent divorce

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher experiences a blast from his past when he takes on restoring a vintage shop till, just like the ones he remembers from his childhood. Janine Savage from Dorset hopes wood aficionado Will Kirk can transform a very special dolls house custom made for her by her grandfather, while the Rev Richard Hayes arrives with a battered leather box containing a compact communion set, hoping its years of use can be made much less evident tghanks to the skills of Suzie Fletcher

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Couple Ian and Ruth run a 150-acre sheep farm in Rhayader, Powys, but don't have their own garden. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich find themselves called upon to create a Beatrix Potter-inspired one in an empty field on a budget of £2,500

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: The presenters look to the future. George Anderson plants up a large container with what he calls a 'bulb lasagne', using different kinds of bulbs to produce layers of colour in the spring. Carole Baxter shows different methods of propagating new plants for next year, Chris Beardshaw plants a new border for year-round colour and Kirsty Wilson reveals why green roofs are the next big thing

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer head to Norfolk, where they attempt to ensnare a large pike - a fish that has eluded them on their trips so far. As they arrive and get their gear together, Bob introduces Paul to the surprisingly varied contents of his car boot, much to Paul's amusement. They gather their equipment and find a decent spot that should give them plenty of chances to catch and make camp for the day. They end their trip with a visit to a local pub, where Bob reveals a hidden trick

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie. A team of three contestants faces a golden wall of cash worth £20,000 and its members try to keep it intact as much of it as possible by correctly answering a series of questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Clevedon, where artist John Webster and globetrotting retiree Caroline Crutchley learn the tricks of the antiques-buying trade to see who can make the most profit

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Coverage of Liberal Democrat leader Ed Davey's speech at the party's conference

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter B. The programme includes clips of Levi Roots preparing beef pepperpot stew, and Nigel Slater cooking a spiced bread and butter pudding

programme.title: Cities: Nature's New Wild
programme.desc: Footage of animals that have made their homes in urban environments around the world. Featuring huge colonies of bats in Adelaide, smooth-coated otters at home in Singapore, and reticulated pythons living on the streets of Bangkok. Narrated by Daniel Kaluuya

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Will Axon and Christina Trevanion assess items at Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, including some intriguing decorative pictures, while Paul Martin looks at the man who saved the hall

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Inner-city farm manager Tom showcases his sheep at the Lambeth Country Show, while beekeeper Paul strives to sell his produce to support local charities close to his heart

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer Paul Rose heads for Swaledale, joining farmers who look after rare upland hay meadows and enters the cake-making contest at Muker Show using a pressure cooker he has taken on a climb of Mount Everest. Paul also goes underground to try and find rare industrial artefacts left behind by lead miners in the 19th century and meets All Creatures Great and Small star Peter Davison

programme.title: Tom Kerridge: Lose Weight for Good
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the chef takes the group of dieters on a river cruise to celebrate their weight loss over the past 12 weeks. Tom prepares a selection of sharing dishes for the special occasion, including couscous, griddled halloumi and Mediterranean vegetables, pulled pork tacos with salad, and falafels with a tzatziki dip

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as family team the Bridges take on murder mystery fans the Whodunnits. The sides must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Printing, Compass, Papermaking, and Gunpowder

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The academic quiz's first-round matches continue, with four students from the University of Warwick pitting their wits against a team from Wolfson College, Oxford in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Shipman Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Chris Wilson re-examines the case of Harold Shipman, exploring how attitudes to the elderly enabled the GP to get away with the murder of hundreds of patients. In the first instalment, he reveals how the doctor's crimes were first discovered and how he was finally apprehended and charged with the murder of 15 of his elderly patients.

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis look back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Nish Kumar, Tiff Stevenson and Tom Allen. As usual, the contestants engage in various rounds of topical stand-up and improvisation as they try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti head to the Andean Cloud Forest of Ecuador to work at Mashpi Lodge, a $10 million modernist hotel featuring an extraordinary gondola cable car that ‘flies' guests one mile through the jungle canopy at a dizzying height

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Action from the fourth match in the five-game series, held at Incora County Ground in Derby

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Paper Boi becomes the hip-hop world's poster-child for toxic masculinity and transphobia after he makes some offensive tweets about Caitlyn Jenner, and ends up on a talk show - but the presenters are mistaken if they think they can get him to apologise

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Paper Boi secures a gig playing a club, but neither he nor Earn are keen on the prospect, especially when they fall foul of a ruthless promoter unwilling to pay his dues and end up hosting the second most popular party in the room

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn accompanies Van to an upmarket Juneteenth soiree, pretending to be a happily married couple with Ivy-League educations to try to help Van secure a networking opportunity with one of the many successful Atlanta power-players in attendance

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith are with four of the UK's leading shepherds and their sheepdogs for this year's Countryfile One Man and His Dog competition, which takes place on a tough course designed to test man and dog in the grounds of historic Hardwick Hall in Derbyshire. The coronavirus means things have to look a little different this year, with just one competitor from each home nation vying for glory. But this socially-distanced shepherding showdown is as testing and competitive as ever. The premier league of shepherding talent from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland must battle it out over two rounds - the 'singles', where they run one dog, and the notoriously difficult 'brace', where they must work two dogs at the same time. Whoever gets the highest combined score will secure the pride of their nation and the coveted title

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the first episode, ambulance crew members Kate and Femi are dispatched to a young man who is struggling to breathe during a severe anxiety attack. Meanwhile, just three hours into the morning shift, control deal with the third assault of the day, having already responded to four stabbings

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein revisit the savage murder of a pub landlady in a sleepy Oxfordshire hamlet in 1922, which led to the unlikely conviction of a 15-year-old boy

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades oversees proceedings as the show's experts restore an 80-year-old banjo, a pair of 1930s rollerskates and a 19th-century wooden salad bowl

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers have a £5,500 budget to create a dramatic Balinese garden full of greenery and tropical plants for newlyweds Peter and Samantha in Cheltenham

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With autumn on the horizon, cameras capture the seasonal planting of bulbs for spring-flowering begins at Longmeadow.. Frances Tophill meets a gardener whose allotment is filled with both traditional and exotic vegetables and Toby Buckland visits The Newt in Somerset, to find out about its chequered garden history and to explore its emergence as one of the UK's most exciting gardens. Plus, another chance to see Mark Lane's visit to Oxfordshire to see how ornamental grasses, perennials and annuals can be used in planting design, to create exciting combinations

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest Super League matches, including Warrington Wolves v Catalans Dragons, and Hull Kingston Rovers v Leeds Rhinos

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Stratford-upon-Avon, where businessman Kamran Osman challenges behaviour consultant Neil Tomalin to buy and sell antiques to try to make the most profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: A widower quits his job in Sydney and buys a house in a remote seaside town in New Zealand, taking his teenage children with him. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman welcomes 10 more hopefuls as the amateur sewing competition returns, with Patrick Grant being joined on the judging team by Esme Young, co-founder of fashion label Swanky Modes and tutor in pattern-cutting at Central Saint Martins art school in London. The first challenge sees the contestants follow a deceptively difficult pattern for a woman's top made up of four pieces, before they have to transform a maternity dress in just 90 mins and fit a skirt to a real model in the final task

programme.title: Cities: Nature's New Wild
programme.desc: Animals that travel in and out of cities in search of food or a safe place for their young. African penguins have found the streets of Cape Town a safe environment from predators, while still providing easy access to their fishing grounds, while in St Lucia, hippos have developed a taste for eating residents' lawns. Black bears roam the streets of cities across North America, seeking out discarded food to help them consume the 20,000 calories a day they need to survive their six-month hibernation

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk, David Harper is excited by a Polynesian war club, while Philip Serrell falls for a sewing machine dating back to 1875. Presenter Paul Martin uncovers the history of the Bedingfeld family, whose members have called Oxburgh home for 500 years - and managed to keep their heads on their shoulders

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Pig farmer Keith and budding herb grower Peter battle the temperamental British weather to get their premium produce to families and friends at Blackheath Farmers' Market

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: Documentary telling the stories of specific bombs dropped on Britain during the Blitz, from their impact on individual lives through to the wider consequences for the Second World War. The first film follows a bomb that fell on Martindale Road in the East End of London on the first night of the Blitz. There was relief that it remained unexploded - but tragedy was soon to follow. The story is told through first-hand testimony, diary excerpts and meticulous records kept at the time

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts and Dr Yasmin Khan reveal how Birmingham's Victorian working class residents made their home city one of the most important industrial centres on the planet. They examine the surviving remains of one of the world's oldest railway terminuses, and discover how many of the thousands of skeletons in the cemetery have evidence of bone damage or amputation, as well as signs of the after-effects of industrial pollution. Plus, a look at the true story behind Peaky Blinders

programme.title: The Shipman Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Chris Wilson examines how the idea of 'a good death' helped Harold Shipman escape detection. Since his victims died painlessly in the comfort of their own home, many family members were too grateful for the lack of suffering to question anything untoward about the doctor's actions. He talks to the family that led a successful campaign to reexamine every death connected to Shipman throughout his medical career - going back to 1974

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Fixing Challenge
programme.desc: Two brothers document their efforts to ensure their father adopts a healthier lifestyle. Security guard Geoff Whitington suffers from a variety of conditions liked to type two diabetes, which nearly resulted in him having to have a foot amputated and which have left him resigned to a premature death. His sons refuse to accept this, and set him a series of challenges to change his lifestyle, with the ultimate goal of cycling 100 miles

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn's jacket goes missing after a house party. Determined to track it down, he retraces his steps, and manages to tie up a few loose ends in the process

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: The return of the comedy drama about a rapper and his manager. As 'Robbin' Season' dawns on Atlanta, things escalate when Earn and Darius pay a visit to Earn's uncle, 'The Alligator Man'. Starring Donald Glover, Brian Tyree Henry and Lakeith Stanfield

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Al and Earn have a day of meetings at the record company but things do not look promising as they have become mysteriously distant. Tracy, fresh out of jail, provides Earn with another way to make some extra cash. Comedy drama, starring Donald Glover, Brian Tyree Henry and Khris Davis

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: More highlights from the past two series of the motoring show, including Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness building their own electric sports cars for the inaugural 24 Minutes of Le Mansfield race. Plus, Chris also takes the electric Porsche Taycan for a spin and the show pays tribute to Colin McRae

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: One-off edition of the observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. The programmes examines how long-serving and newly-qualified Northumbria Police officers respond to the changing world of modern crime, including managing online sex offenders and the threat of firearms. In one of Northumbria's newest departments - the Paedophile Online Investigation Team - Detective Constable Dave Kernaghan is managing a huge rise in new cases, but as he continues to investigate the escalating number of jobs, the work itself takes a toll as he assesses the impact this is having on his own life

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Chris Bavin takes a look at claims that diet drinks could be making people gain weight, Gloria Hunniford finds out whether all carbs should be avoided or if some are the key to a healthy lifestyle and Danny Crates looks at whether greasy and sugary foods affect people's skin

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team takes on the challenge of restoring an 18th-century French clock by a renowned maker, a 1960s radiogram, and a Victorian botanical microscope

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers create a practical but fun garden for a St Albans family, with play structures for the children and a place to grow fruit and vegetables

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The farmers settle their livestock into winter housing as the first frosts come in. In the Lake District, vet Dani prepares to perform pregnancy tests on all her cows - while six months pregnant herself. The outcome of the tests will determine the future income of the farm. High on the uplands of Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan prepare to undertake a new venture, and in Aberdeenshire, Isla and George fight to save a dangerously dehydrated deer calf

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin heads to Exeter to shows former DJ Tricia O'Neall and teacher Phil Briggs the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: The septic tank in the Turners' rickety house explodes, and to make matters worse, Shay and Arlo are having difficulties at school. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman sets the remaining contestants three challenges designed to test different fabric handling skills on children's clothing. Firstly, they must carefully follow a pattern to make baby grows from stretch cotton jersey and then show they can handle slippery satin and chiffon by transforming an adult bridesmaid's dress into a garment for a boy or a girl. Finally, they have to create perfectly fitted woollen capes, before judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young decide who leaves the competition

programme.title: Cities: Nature's New Wild
programme.desc: Animals displaced from their habitats by human expansion, including elephants in Sri Lanka and capuchin monkeys on the Pacific coast of Costa Rica. The programme also examines situations where humans have learned to live alongside their animal neighbours, including a heron that has been visiting an Amsterdam house twice a day for 17 years and a project to preserve manatees in Florida that has saved them from being an endangered species

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Natasha Raskin search for antiques at Watts Gallery in Surrey, and Paul Martin uncovers the story of the ceramicist and designer Mary Watts

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chris Hollins, Gregg Wallace, Holly Walsh and Charlene White test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Veterinary surgeon Robin goes head to head with experienced breeder, 81 year-old Di, with their diminutive Dexter cattle in the National Competition at the Royal Norfolk Show

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on wartime Hull and the consequences of a specific bomb that fell on the city's 6th Avenue, flattening two houses and changing the lives of two families for ever. The Taylors lost baby Peter and 14-year-old Vera was very badly burned. Next door, Mrs Owens had put three of her children under the stairs for safety, but they didn't survive. Contributors include Mrs Owens' daughter Janet, who was born after the war and has many questions about what happened to her siblings. Here she meets the surviving Taylor sisters to talk about the event

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary explores how families use food to create their own sense of home, including a father and daughter who have taken their home cooking to the streets with their own food van. Later in the programme, Mary makes some of her own family favourites, including a hearty bolognaise bake with a burrata and heritage tomato salad, and a paneer and roast vegetable curry

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the fourth episode, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite chocolate recipes, including her 'Money Can't Buy You Happiness' brownies, a chocolate flan, and snack sized madeleines

programme.title: The Shipman Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The final edition opens by looking at the first report of the public inquiry into the deaths of Harold Shipman's patients, concluding that while working as a GP he had murdered 260 of them. Chris Wilson explores the reaction to the staggering total, exploring how attention began to focus on the killer as experts and commentators came up with a range of psychological profiles and theories to try to understand why one, seemingly ordinary doctor had killed so many people

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Leading performers on the stand-up circuit share their light-hearted views on modern life in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. Tonight, the host Henning Wehn welcomes Lucy Porter and Guz Khan to the stage

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Action from the final match of the five-game series, held at Incora County Ground in Derby, as both sides looked for a morale-boosting victory ahead of the ICC T20 World Cup

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: When Earn receives a cheque, he's determined to treat his girlfriend, Van, to a fancy night out. Meanwhile, Paper Boi and Darius head into the recording studio with Clark County, who is considered one of the hottest rappers in town, to see what new 'hot tracks' they can collaborate on

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn discovers just how weird white people can be when he accompanies Van to Helen, Georgia, to attend the traditional Germanic festival celebration known as Fasnacht that she frequented as a child

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Al's trip to get a haircut turns into a whistle-stop tour round Atlanta in his barber's car. Yet all he really wanted was to have a trim. Comedy drama, starring Brian Tyree Henry

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: At Swanage lifeboat station, brothers Matt and Gavin, following in their father's footsteps, race to rescue a pair of novice paddleboarders before they are swept out into the busy shipping lanes. On the north east coast, the Withernsea crew scours the North Sea for a local fisherman and friend who is missing at sea, while in Blackpool, a volunteer's wedding day is interrupted by an urgent call for help

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges a team of doctors to diagnose a young woman struggling with dramatic weight gain and hair loss, and another who has suddenly lost her sight in one eye. In the last few years, 26-year-old Hayley has gained 20kg and gone from a size 4-6 to a size 18-20, without changing her diet. She also struggles with muscle weakness and constant fatigue, and her hair has started falling out. While the experts begin their investigations into Hayley's complex case, Michael introduces them to another young woman who is struggling with frightening symptoms. In 2017, while on a camping holiday with her mum in the Lake District, Lotte developed a severe headache that has never stopped since, and she is now unable to see anything out of her left eye

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Timothy Spall: Somewhere at Sea
programme.desc: The actor and his wife Shane embark on a journey from Cardiff to Clydebank in their barge the Princess Matilda. The first leg of the voyage begins smoothly as they travel to Milford Haven - but a bungled departure leaves them marooned en route to Fishguard. Shane also gets to make an emotional return to Aberystwyth, and the couple receive a pleasant surprise when they visit the coastal village of Porthdinllaen

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene depicting the beauty of a shimmering pool gently cradled by a soft, beautiful, bright forest

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Nicky Philipps, an artist who has previously painted portraits of the royal family, seeks help in determining whether a mysterious painting housed at Picton Castle in Pembrokeshire is a genuine work by celebrated French Impressionist Pierre Auguste Renoir. Picton Castle is the ancestral home of Nicky's family, and her aunt used to tell an intriguing tale that the picture in question came from Claude Monet's house in Giverny, and was originally a gift given to the artist by Renoir himself. Fiona Bruce heads to Monet's home to determine whether this rumour is true, and investigates contrasting academic opinions on whether the painting is genuine. Meanwhile, Philip Mould travels to the Parisian suburb of Argenteuil and to Berlin to make use of cutting-edge technology to determine whether the pigments in Nicky's picture match up with those listed as being used by Renoir

programme.title: The Secret History of Writing
programme.desc: Lydia Wilson examines the changing technology of writing through the centuries, looking at how it has shaped the course of history and been a factor in the rise and fall of empires

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: Documentary about the execution of Charles I, beginning by following the events of Christmas 1648. The king was visited at Windsor by the Earl of Denbigh, seeking to broker terms between the imprisoned monarch and an increasingly hardline parliament. When his visit was rejected, new legislation is drawn up to try the king for treason

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating handcrafted objects made under the royal warrant, beginning by following the creation of a Wedgwood panther vase by artisan potters. The programme also takes a look at how this quintessentially British company is reinventing itself for the 21st century under new, foreign ownership

programme.title: Sold! Inside the World's Biggest Auction House
programme.desc: The second of a two-part documentary going behind the scenes at Christie's, where Global President Jussi Pylkkanen and his team continue with their big push into Asia. As the company prepares for its sale to celebrate its 250th anniversary, an umbrella stand turns out to be worth millions and a Rubens appears that has not been seen in public for 150 years

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?

programme.title: The Secret History of Writing
programme.desc: Lydia Wilson examines the changing technology of writing through the centuries, looking at how it has shaped the course of history and been a factor in the rise and fall of empires

programme.title: Timothy Spall: Somewhere at Sea
programme.desc: The Princess Matilda sails into Liverpool, but Tim has a difficult time reaching shore as he struggles to navigate around tankers and ferries. The barge then heads to Glasson Dock in Lancashire, where the hapless actor has more trouble with buoys, before a misunderstanding results in Matilda spending a night stranded on the Irish Sea. Tim and Shane also visit Piel Island near Barrow-in-Furness, which has the unusual honour of having its own king and queen

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a fast flowing little waterfall, running through lush green meadow grass in the evening light

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: Following the astonishing wildlife, landscapes and people of the world's longest river, the Nile, beginning in the tropical mountain range of the Rwenzoris, home to one of the world's strangest birds, the shoebill stork. Then, cameras follow the Nile's journey as it travels through the wild Savannah lands of Uganda, before reaching the Ethiopian highlands where it receives an injection of water far to the East

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The author and adventurer continues his Australian journey in Kakadu, the country's largest national park, where he works through the night with a team committed to culling the poisonous feral cane toads that are threatening the region's wildlife. He then learns how to survive off the land using ancient Aboriginal knowledge as he heads out on patrol with a unique military unit in the remote northern Bush, before attempting to catch box jellyfish on Cape York Peninsula with a group of scientists who believe the species' deadly venom may have medicinal qualities. Finally, Simon flies 100 miles out over the ocean to watch a large tanker being guided through the Great Barrier Reef

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: Three-part documentary focusing on the monarch's final days. In the second episode, the country prepares for the unprecedented trial of the King. Although the consensus is that the King is a tyrant, only half of the commissioners appointed actually attend the meetings at Westminster Palace, in fear of being indicted as traitors. On the 20th of January 1649, the three day trial begins, and the King and the Lord President John Bradshaw become embroiled in a battle of wills

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: A look at the work of shoemakers John Lobb, which has produced hand-crafted footwear for gentlemen since the 19th century and boasts Royal Warrants from Prince Philip and Prince Charles. The company uses methods that have barely changed since it was founded, from pencilled outlines on brown paper, to the cutting and stitching of leather and the final polishing. Zoe Wanamaker narrates

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Rid of the human remains, Sandra's dig is disturbed by an intolerant tribal elder, while Jay presses Simon on his suspicions

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Jay refuses to accept that the drugs case has been solved. Meanwhile, the forensics on the body in the bag add to Fran's distress

programme.title: Live Women's FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Live coverage of a semi-final (Kick-off TBA), featuring Brighton & Hove Albion or Birmingham City v Everton or Chelsea, or Arsenal or Tottenham Hotspur v Leicester City or Manchester City. The competition got under way more than 12 months ago, and the teams are now one win away from a place in the Wembley final on October 31 and the chance to lift the trophy claimed by Manchester City in the 2018/19 campaign

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Parkrun started out in London as a simple idea to get friends together for a time trial and a coffee, but now attracts more than 235,000 participants around the globe every weekend. In this film, double Olympic gold medallist Kelly Holmes explores the popularity of the events and the mental health benefits of running in the build-up to a new Parkrun in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Charles I: Killing a King
programme.desc: The king continues to publicly dispute the legitimacy of the high court but after two days of witness testimonies, the evidence against him is overwhelming. When Charles enters the courtroom on the third day, he is prepared to compromise but it is too late - he is found guilty of treason and is sentenced to be executed

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: In a small workshop in a country house in Wiltshire, silversmith Simon Benney makes distinctive jewellery and household objects for the royal family and private clients. Simon is following in the footsteps of his father, Gerald, who revolutionised the design of British silverware in the post-war era. This film follows the making of an engraved, gold and diamond pendant by Simon, featuring a trademark enamel finish using techniques his father learnt from Faberge

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of the craftspeople behind the manufacture, maintenance and restoration of Steinway pianos, from the German factory workers bending the frames and polishing the veneers to longstanding British restorer Jeff about to retire from the company. Steinway supplies pianos to the royal household as well as many leading performers, and the film goes behind the scenes as renowned pianist Lang Lang prepares for a concert at the Royal Albert Hall

programme.title: The Secret History of Writing
programme.desc: Tracing the history of the written word. The second episode examines how the changing technology of writing has shaped the course of world history

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: It’s the zoo’s grand reopening and the boys are preparing to open the gates to the public for the first time. The night before, they were rudely awoken, and Moto the lion took the opportunity to investigate what the animals get up to during the dead of night. Cam draws the short straw and has to clean out the camel enclosure, while Billy the ocelot is given a pungent treat to play with and the boys recall the extraordinary story of how the zoo was started. Meanwhile, the brothers catch up on when they met with rangers on the front line training to intercept and arrest wildlife poachers.
